content: The prompt-reviewer rejected the initial 67-agent prompt-writer output (cycle 1) but approved all subsequent submissions (cycles 2, 3) on first attempt. This 'reject-once-then-approve' pattern suggests the first-pass writing of a large agent family has predictable quality issues that are corrected on rewrite, while incremental fix passes (re-entry cycles) are reliably approvable.
sourceEvidence: prompt-reviewer cycle 1: 'rejected' (02:15). cycle 2: 'approved' (06:30). cycle 3 re-entry: 'approved' (17:26). cycle 3 re-entry 2: 'approved' (19:03). cycle 3 re-entry 3: 'approved' (21:19).

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
